clinical_history: Why is this young individual with cocaine-use disorder in the emergency department ?
diagnosis: He is having an evolving anteroseptal myocardial infarction attributable to cocaine use.  There are Q waves in V2-V3 with ST segment elevation in V2-V5 associated with T-wave inversion.  Also noted are biphasic T-waves in the inferior leads.  These multiple abnormalities suggest occlusion of a large left anterior descending (LAD) artery that wraps around the apex of the heart (or of multivessel coronary artery disease).
